questions.question: What is a quadratic equation?
questions.correct_answer: An equation of the form ax² + bx + c = 0, where a ≠ 0
questions.explanation: The highest power of the variable is 2.

questions.question: What is the degree of a quadratic equation?
questions.explanation: The highest power of x is 2.

questions.question: What is the discriminant of a quadratic equation?
questions.explanation: It determines the nature of roots.

questions.question: When are the roots real and distinct?
When are the roots real and equal?
When does a quadratic equation have no real roots?
Which method always works for solving quadratic equations?
questions.explanation: It can be used in all cases.

questions.question: What is the graph of a quadratic equation?
What do the roots of a quadratic equation represent on the graph?
questions.explanation: Roots are where the graph cuts the x-axis.

questions.question: How many roots can a quadratic equation have at most?

notes.title: Standard Form
notes.content: Every quadratic equation can be written as ax² + bx + c = 0.

notes.title: Nature of Roots
notes.content: The discriminant decides the nature of roots.

notes.title: Graphical Meaning
notes.content: Roots correspond to x-intercepts of the parabola.

notes.title: Solving Methods
notes.content: Factorisation and quadratic formula are commonly used methods.

notes.title: Applications
notes.content: Quadratic equations appear in area and motion problems.

facts.fact: A quadratic equation always has two roots (real or imaginary).
If a > 0, the parabola opens upward.
If a < 0, the parabola opens downward.
